_comment: Kurzfassungen der Regel-/Zauber-Texte fuer die Rueckseite und den Plan. 'overrides' = vom Nutzer kuratierte Kartentexte (max. 2 Zeilen / 150 Zeichen), gewinnen IMMER, auch gegen den Text aus der Export-JSON. 'summaries' = von Hand gepflegte Kurztexte, NUR Fallback ohne Export-Text (Schluessel = exakter Name). 'learned' = frueher automatisch gemerkt, waechst nicht mehr; bei Bedarf korrigieren oder nach 'summaries' verschieben. Ein Eintrag wird ueberall identisch verwendet (Karte + Plan). 'front_highlights' = Kampf-Sonderregeln, die als Auf-einen-Blick-Zeile auf die VORDERSEITE kommen; Wert = Gruppe: 'charge' (wenn du angreifst), 'charged' (wenn du angegriffen wirst), '' (immer/Nahkampf).

overrides.Swiftstride: Unit increases max Charge range +3" and adds a D6 to Charge, Flee or Pursuit rolls.
overrides.Magic Resistance: Casting roll of enemy spells against a model or unit with this rule suffers the bracketed modifier (-X).
overrides.Hammer of Sigmar: Until next turn, the model and a friendly unit (cmd range) re-rolls To Hit and To Wound rolls of natural 1.
overrides.Shield of Faith: Until next turn, the model and a friendly unit (cmd range) gains a 5+ Ward save against shooting attacks.
overrides.Arise!: Cmd sub-phase, not in combat: Ld test → one Undead unit within 12" recovers Wounds: infantry Lvl+D3, cavalry Lvl+1, monstrous Lvl, behemoth 1.
overrides.Armour Bane: A natural 6 To Wound improves the weapon's AP by the bracketed value (X).
overrides.Assorted Weapons: Each crew member carries a different weapon; roll separate dice batches per weapon and declare which attacks they represent.
overrides.Chariot Runners: Friendly chariots may see and move through the skirmishing unit (nudge runners aside); chariots within 1" count for unit coherency.
overrides.Choppas: On the charge: re-roll To Wound rolls of natural 1 and +1 AP. Non-magical weapons only, not the mount.
overrides.Djaf's Incantation of Cursed Blades: Until next turn, friendly Nehekharan Undead units in cmd range re-roll To Hit rolls of natural 1.
overrides.Earthen Ramparts: Until next turn: target friendly unit gains a 5+ Ward save and counts as behind a defended obstacle, but cannot march or charge.
overrides.Fast Cavalry: Open Order unit (all models with this rule) may Quick Turn even after marching.
overrides.Fear: Charging a Fear-causer with higher US: Ld test or the charge fails. In combat vs one: Ld test or -1 To Hit. One test per turn; Fear-causers immune.
overrides.Finest Warhorses: Charge, Flee and Pursuit rolls: re-roll dice showing a natural 1 (before discarding).
overrides.Fire & Flee: Charge reaction: Stand & Shoot, then flee rolling 2D6 and discarding the lowest. If charger's M ≥ distance: only Hold or Flee.
overrides.First Charge: First successful charge of the game: the charge target is Disrupted until the end of that Combat phase.
overrides.From Beneath the Sands: Cmd sub-phase, not in combat: Ld test → place one reserved Undead Ambusher unit within 12" (not within 6" of enemies); no charge this turn.
overrides.Impetuous: If the unit could declare a charge: Ld test — failed = it MUST charge, passed = acts normally.
overrides.Khsar's Incantation of the Desert Wind: Cast 6+: one friendly Undead unit in cmd range gains Reserve Move; cast 10+: all of them. Until end of turn.
overrides.Master of Ballistics: Once per turn (not fleeing/in combat): a friendly war machine in cmd range uses this model's BS or re-rolls one Artillery die.
overrides.Motley Crew: Mixed unit: roll separate dice per weapon type; armour value = majority; shooting casualties spread evenly, combat casualties from majority.
overrides.Order of the Fiery Heart: Hatred (Wizards, Orcs & Goblins), +1 Initiative in first combat round; Master & Inner Circle re-roll natural 1 To Hit/To Wound in combat.
overrides.Prayers of Sigmar: Cmd sub-phase: Ld test → chant ONE of the Prayers below. Effects are not cumulative; announce which unit is affected.
overrides.Regeneration: After losing a Wound: roll a D6, on (X)+ the Wound is recovered (still counts for combat result). Armour modifiers don't apply.
overrides.Reserve Move: If the unit did not charge, march or flee: extra move at the end of its Shooting phase (manoeuvre normally, no march).
overrides.Shield of the Lady: In a unit (US 10+, Chivalrous Vow) the character may retire to the rear: no attacks by or against them, still confers Ld/rules, may cast.
overrides.Shieldwall: Once per game, when charged: Close Order unit using shields may Give Ground instead of Fall Back in Good Order.
overrides.Spirit Leech: Until end of turn: target enemy unit (even in combat) suffers -2 Ld (min 2) and cannot use the General's Inspiring Presence.
overrides.Stubborn: First Break test of the game: unit may skip it and automatically Fall Back in Good Order instead. Not conferred by or to joined characters.
overrides.Stupidity: Start of Turn: Ld test or until next turn the unit cannot move/shoot/cast/dispel and must Hold if charged. Contagious to unit/mount.
overrides.Temperamental: Natural 1 on Steam Power or Charge roll: choose — lose 1 Wound OR halt for the rest of the turn. Double 1: lose 1 Wound AND halt.
overrides.Travel Mystical Pathway: Target friendly unit (not fled/moved) is placed anywhere within 12" of its position, not within 6" of enemies; cannot move again this phase.
overrides.Warband: +Ld equal to current Rank Bonus (max Ld 10; not for Restraint/Impetuous tests); majority with this rule: re-roll Charge rolls.
overrides.The Knight's Vow: No Panic test when friendly Peasantry within 6" are destroyed or flee through. Cannot mix with Peasantry characters/units.
overrides.Peasantry: Within 6" of a non-fleeing model with a Knight's/Questing/Grail Vow: may use its Ld. Standard never counts as a trophy; only joins Peasantry.
overrides.Terror: When this unit declares a charge: the target must pass a Ld test or Flee. Chargers against it test as for Fear. Immune to Fear/Terror.

summaries.General: Inspiring Presence: Unless the General is fleeing, all friendly units within his Command range may use his Leadership instead of their own.
summaries.Swiftstride: +3" maximum charge range; rolls an extra D6 for Charge, Flee and Pursuit moves and discards the lowest die.
summaries.Terror: When a Terror-causer declares a charge, the target must pass a Leadership test or Flee. If the winning side of a combat includes a Terror-causer, each losing unit applies -1 Leadership to its Break test. Also causes Fear.
summaries.Fly: Moves by flying up to the bracketed distance, ignoring terrain and intervening models.
summaries.Large Target: Always in line of sight: LoS can be drawn over or through other (non-Large) units, and vice versa. Gets no cover benefit. A unit shooting at a Large Target may fire with one additional rank.
summaries.Counter Charge: Charge reaction usable only when charged in the front arc by cavalry, a chariot or a monster. Pivot to face the charger and move D3+1" toward it; both units then count as having charged (so you also gain charge bonuses). If the gap is less than the charger's Movement, must Hold or Flee instead. One Counter Charge per turn.
summaries.Stubborn: The first failed Break test may be ignored: the unit automatically Falls Back in Good Order instead, no matter how badly it lost the combat.
summaries.Fear: To charge a Fear-causer with higher Unit Strength you must first pass a Leadership test or the charge fails. If engaged with one and the test is failed, your attacks on it take -1 To Hit. One Fear test per turn.
summaries.Impetuous: If the unit is able to declare a charge, it must pass a Leadership test or it is forced to charge.
summaries.Fast Cavalry: In Open Order, may make its Quick Turn even after marching.
summaries.Immune To Psychology: If most models have it, the unit auto-passes Fear, Panic and Terror tests — but may not choose Flee as a charge reaction.
summaries.Unbreakable: Never takes Break tests: on losing a combat round it automatically Gives Ground instead of breaking.
summaries.Feel No Pain: 6+ Ward save against non-magical wounds (5+ if the attack was Strength 5 or higher).
summaries.Regeneration: After a wound is lost, roll a D6: on the bracketed value or higher the wound is regained (still counts for combat result). Armour modifiers do not affect it.
summaries.Stupidity: Test Leadership each Start of Turn (unless fleeing or in combat). On a fail: cannot move (except flee), shoot or cast, and must Hold if charged, until its next turn. Contagious to joined characters/mounts.
summaries.Move Through Cover: No Movement penalty for difficult/dangerous terrain, and may re-roll 1s on Dangerous Terrain tests.
summaries.Vanguard: After deployment, may make a free move (no marching); cannot charge on turn one.
summaries.Scouts: Deployed last, anywhere more than 12" from any enemy; cannot charge on turn one.
summaries.Ambushers: May be held in reserve; from round 2 roll each Start of Turn (4+ = arrives) and enter from any board edge, more than 8" from enemies. Automatic by round 5.
summaries.Ancestral Grudge: Has Hatred (enemy characters); also grants it to a joined unit of Longbeards or Hammerers.
summaries.First Charge: On its first successful charge of the game, the charge target becomes Disrupted until the end of that Combat phase.
summaries.Finest Warhorses: When making a Charge, Flee or Pursuit roll, may re-roll natural 1s (before discarding any required dice).
summaries.Magic Resistance: Enemy spells (including Bound Spells) targeting the unit suffer the bracketed penalty to their Casting roll. Not cumulative — use the highest.
summaries.Detachment: May be fielded as a detachment of a parent regiment (see rulebook).
summaries.Drilled: Free 'redress the ranks' before moving, and may march within 8" of an enemy without a Leadership test.
summaries.Veteran: If most models have it, the unit may re-roll failed Leadership tests (a Break test is not a Leadership test).
summaries.Reserve Move: If it did not charge, march or flee, may make an extra move (no march) at the end of its Shooting phase.
summaries.Fire & Flee: Charge reaction for missile units: Stand & Shoot at the charger, then Flee, rolling 2D6 and discarding the lowest for the Flee move. If the gap is under the charger's Movement, must Hold or Flee.
summaries.Temperamental: Steam Tank: a natural 1 on a Steam Power or Charge roll forces a mishap (lose 1 Wound, or halt for the turn); double 1s = lose a Wound and halt.
summaries.Steam Power: Steam Tank cannot march; instead add a Steam Power roll (2D6, discard the lowest) to its Movement.
summaries.Unstoppable Assault: On a turn it charged, each attack that causes an unsaved wound grants an extra attack (which can chain). Stomp wounds do not count.
summaries.Shieldwall: Once per game, if charged while in Close Order using shields, may Give Ground instead of Falling Back in Good Order.
summaries.Loner: Cannot be the General; cannot join (or be joined within) a unit that lacks this rule.
summaries.Resolute: Suffers -1 to any Flee or Pursuit roll (to a minimum of 1).
summaries.Furious Charge: On a turn it made a charge move of 3" or more, +1 Attack.
summaries.Stomp Attacks: Automatic hits dealt last in combat (after all attacks, even Initiative 1) by a model in base contact, using its unmodified Strength. Number = bracketed value.
summaries.Impact Hits: A charging model that moved 3"+ deals the bracketed number of automatic hits at the start of the combat (before challenges), using its unmodified Strength.
summaries.Indomitable: Reduces wounds suffered from the Unstable rule by the bracketed value. Not cumulative — the unit uses the highest.
summaries.Nehekharan Undead: Undead: cannot march (unless flying). Always has Fear, Immune to Psychology, Unbreakable and Unstable. Cannot mix with non-Undead in a unit.
summaries.Arise!: Command sub-phase (if not in combat): pass a Leadership test to heal a friendly Nehekharan Undead unit within 12". Wounds restored scale with the caster's Wizard level and the unit's troop type.
summaries.From Beneath the Sands: Command sub-phase (if not in combat): pass a Leadership test to summon a reserved friendly Nehekharan Undead Ambusher unit within 12" (not within 6" of enemies). It cannot charge that turn.
summaries.Peasantry: Within 6" of a non-fleeing friendly model with a Knight's, Questing or Grail Vow, may use that model's Leadership. Only joins/joined-by units that also have Peasantry.
summaries.The Knight's Vow: No Panic test when a friendly Peasantry unit within 6" is destroyed, or when one flees through it. Cannot be mixed with Peasantry characters/units.
summaries.My Will Be Done: Command sub-phase: pass a Leadership test to grant this character, mount and joined unit one buff until next turn — +D3 Movement, +1 Weapon Skill, or +D3 Initiative (max 10). Not cumulative.
summaries.Crushing Weight: The War Wagon's Stomp attacks have AP -1, and it treats low linear obstacles as dangerous terrain rather than impassable.
summaries.Hatred: Re-roll failed To Hit rolls against the hated enemy (shown in brackets) during the first round of combat.
summaries.Waaagh!: Once per game, Command sub-phase: pass a Leadership test so this character, mount and joined Orc unit may re-roll natural-1 To Hit and gain +1 combat result, until next turn.
summaries.Choppas: On a turn it charged, re-roll natural-1 To Wound and improve weapon AP by 1. Non-magical weapons only; not the mount.
summaries.Warband: Unless fleeing, gains +Leadership equal to its current Rank Bonus (max 10), and may re-roll its Charge roll if most models have it. Does not apply to Restraint or Impetuous tests.
summaries.Big 'Uns: In combat: +1 Strength and gains Armour Bane (1).
summaries.Netters: When its combat is chosen, roll a D6 to entangle an enemy unit it is fighting: 2+ that enemy takes -1 Strength until end of the Combat phase (on a 1 it entangles itself). Not cumulative.
summaries.Release the Fanatics!: Hidden Night Goblin Fanatics: released from the unit during a Start of Turn, then move 2D6" (random direction) each Compulsory Moves, dealing D6 S5 AP-3 hits to any unit moved through. Removed on a double move roll or in terrain.
summaries.Ignore Goblin Panic: No Panic test when a friendly Goblin unit within 6" is destroyed, Breaks and flees, or flees through it.
summaries.Tusker Charge: On a turn it charged, the War Boar's tusks are S+1 with AP -1 (the boar's own attacks only, not rider or crew).
summaries.Armoured Hide: Improves the model's (and its rider's) armour value by the bracketed amount. A model with no armour counts as 7+ for this.
summaries.Feigned Flight: If it Flees (or Fire & Flee) as a charge reaction, it automatically rallies at the end of its move.
summaries.Timmm-Berrr!: When reduced to 0 Wounds it topples: units in base contact in the chosen arc take D6 hits at its Strength, AP -1, then it is removed.
summaries.Doom Diver: Fires as a Bombardment, but after the scatter you may shift the template up to D3" in any direction (fired indirectly: move it Artillery dice minus D3 inches).
summaries.Motley Crew: The unit may mix models with different weapons/armour: use the majority's armour value, roll each model's attacks separately, and spread casualties as described.
summaries.Chariot Runners: Friendly chariots can draw line of sight and move through this Skirmish-formation unit (nudge runners aside as needed); friendly chariots within 1" count for unit coherency.
summaries.Fanatical Zeal: Can only be joined by a Lector or Priest of Sigmar, who counts as Unbreakable while they remain with the unit.
summaries.Close Order: May adopt Close Order formation (ranked-up; benefits from rank bonus, but more exposed to shooting).
summaries.Open Order: May adopt Open Order formation (looser ranks; better manoeuvrability).
summaries.Skirmishers: May adopt Skirmish formation (loose, no rank bonus; harder to shoot).
summaries.Dispersed Formation: In Skirmish formation, Pegasus Knights need only stay within 2" of another model (instead of 1").
summaries.Horde: Increases the unit's maximum Rank Bonus by one.
summaries.Magical Attacks: Attacks/hits from this model or weapon count as Magical (all spells and magic-item hits are too).
summaries.Battle Standard Bearer: Grants +1 combat result (stacks with other standards). Friendly units within Command range may re-roll failed Panic/Rally tests and re-roll the 2D6 on a Break test (keep the second roll). Lost if the bearer dies.
summaries.War Banner: +1 combat result point for the unit carrying it.
summaries.Levies: Cannot use the General's Inspiring Presence or a Battle Standard's Hold Your Ground. Other units take no Panic test when a Levy unit Breaks and flees.
summaries.Flammable: Cannot make a Regeneration save against wounds from Flaming attacks.
summaries.Dwarf Crafted: No -1 To Hit penalty when making a Stand & Shoot charge reaction.
summaries.Gromril Armour: May re-roll natural 1s on Armour Save rolls.
summaries.Gromril Weapons: A single ordinary hand weapon gains AP -1 (not when using two weapons, other weapons, or a runed weapon).
summaries.Fear of Elves: Elves of any type cause Fear in this model.
summaries.Zealot: Can only join a unit with the Fanatical Zeal special rule.
summaries.Stoic Defenders: On a turn it was charged, +1 Initiative and +1 Attack.
summaries.Royal Guard: One Hammerer unit per King/Thane; while a King or Thane has joined them, any model in the unit may issue and accept challenges.
summaries.Borne Aloft: Shieldbearers: the character plus three retainers share one base as a single model (Split Profile, Cavalry); otherwise heavy infantry.
summaries.Dive Bomb: Once per game, fly over an enemy unit not in combat: it suffers D6 S3 AP-1 hits per model that passed over it (each natural 1 instead costs this unit a Wound).
summaries.Handgun Drill: Once per game, each State Missile detachment may fire with one additional rank.
summaries.Stable Firing Platform: The War Wagon crew may shoot even if the War Wagon marched this turn.
summaries.Clouds of Shoot & Smoke: On foot, counts as behind full cover while within 3" of a friendly war machine.
summaries.Armour Bane: On a natural 6 To Wound, improve the weapon's AP by the bracketed value.
summaries.Khopesh: A single ordinary hand weapon gains AP -1 (not when using two weapons, other weapons, or a mount).
summaries.Shield of the Lady: While in a unit (Unit Strength 10+ with a Chivalrous Vow), may 'retire' to the rear at any time: makes and receives no attacks, but still grants its Leadership and special rules and may cast as if in the front rank.
summaries.Spirit Leech: Target enemy unit takes -2 Leadership (min 2) and cannot use the General's Inspiring Presence, until end of turn. May target a unit in combat.
summaries.Order of the Fiery Heart: Joined only by an Order Grand/Chapter Master, Lector or Priest of Sigmar. Gains Hatred (enemy Wizards, Orcs & Goblins) and +1 Initiative in the first combat round; Order characters and Inner Circle Knights also re-roll natural-1 To Hit/To Wound in combat.
summaries.Master of Ballistics: Once per turn a friendly Empire war machine within Command range may use this model's Ballistic Skill or re-roll one Artillery dice (not while this model is fleeing or in combat).
summaries.Assorted Weapons: Each War Wagon crewman carries a different unique weapon; missile crew may shoot, and in combat each may use either their unique or hand weapon. Roll separate dice batches per weapon.
summaries.Prayers of Sigmar: Command sub-phase: pass a Leadership test to chant one Prayer — Hammer of Sigmar, Shield of Faith or Soulfire. Effects on a given unit are not cumulative.
summaries.Hammer of Sigmar: Until your next turn, this model, its mount and one friendly unit in Command range may re-roll natural-1 To Hit/To Wound in combat.
summaries.Shield of Faith: Until your next turn, this model and one friendly unit in Command range gain a 5+ Ward save against wounds in the Shooting phase.
summaries.Earthen Ramparts: Until your next turn, target friendly unit gains a 5+ Ward save and counts as behind a defended low obstacle if charged — but cannot march or charge.
summaries.Travel Mystical Pathway: If the target friendly unit hasn't moved and isn't fleeing, teleport it anywhere within 12" of its position (not within 6" of enemies). It cannot move again this phase.
summaries.Khsar's Incantation of the Desert Wind: Cast 6+: one friendly Nehekharan Undead unit in Command range gains Reserve Move; cast 10+: all such units do. Lasts until end of turn.
summaries.Djaf's Incantation of Cursed Blades: Until your next turn, friendly Nehekharan Undead units in the caster's Command range may re-roll natural-1 To Hit.
summaries.Blessings of the Lady: Bretonnia may give up the first-turn roll-off (opponent goes first) to grant models with this rule a 6+ Ward save (5+ vs Strength 5 or higher). Lost by any model that flees or refuses a challenge.
summaries.Rallying Cry: Command sub-phase (if not in combat): a fleeing friendly unit in Command range immediately makes a Rally test (it may still rally normally later if it fails).
summaries.The Grail Vow: Grants Immune to Psychology, Magical Attacks and Stubborn, plus the Blessings of the Lady (no need to pray). Cannot refuse a challenge; cannot join Peasantry units.
summaries.Grail Pendant: Grail Vow models only. One use: when the wearer would lose their last Wound, on a 2+ it is not lost.
summaries.Lore of the Lady: May swap a randomly-generated spell for either its Lore's signature spell or a spell from the Lore of the Lady.
summaries.The Lady's Wrath: Remains in play: the caster, mount and joined unit gain +1 Strength (max 10) and +1 weapon Armour Piercing.
summaries.Burning Gaze: Draw a 5D6" line from the caster's base; every enemy model under it takes a Strength 4 hit with no armour save (Ward/Regeneration allowed).
summaries.The Lady's Gift: Cast 7+: target friendly unit gains Regeneration (6+); cast 10+: Regeneration (5+). May target a unit in combat.
summaries.Pillar of Fire: Remains in play: a 3" template (difficult terrain) placed within 12", scattering D6" each Start of Turn. Enemies it moves over or that move through take D3+3 Strength 3 AP-2 Flaming hits.
summaries.Curse of Cowardly Flight: Target enemy unit must take a Panic test at once — even units that normally auto-pass must test, and Give Ground if they fail.
summaries.Curse of the Necropolis: If this model is slain by an enemy attack, that unit takes a Leadership test; on a fail it suffers D3 Strength 2 hits.
summaries.Lore of Nehekhara: May swap a randomly-generated spell for either its Lore's signature spell or a spell from the Lore of Nehekhara.
summaries.Usekhp's Incantation of Desiccation: Until end of turn, target enemy unit takes -1 Strength and -1 Toughness (min 1). Casting it removes any earlier Hex on the target.
summaries.Deathly Cabal: Until your next turn, the caster and joined unit gain a 6+ Ward save vs non-magical attacks and gain Fear (or Terror if they already cause Fear).
summaries.Spiritual Vortex: Remains in play: a stationary 5" template (dangerous terrain) within 12". Enemy units within 8" take -1 Leadership (min 2) and cannot use their General's Inspiring Presence.
summaries.Curse of Years: Until your next turn, target enemy unit takes -1 Movement, -1 Weapon Skill and -1 Toughness (min 1). Casting it removes any earlier Hex on the target.
summaries.Spectral Steed: Remains in play: target friendly infantry character gains Ethereal and Fly (10).
summaries.Dwellers Bellow: Place a 3" template over a unit the caster is fighting; it scatters D3+1". Models under its final position take a Strength 3 hit.
summaries.Dry as Dust: For each unsaved wound from a Flaming Attack, the opponent rolls a D6: on 4+ this model loses one additional Wound.
summaries.Cleaving Blow: On a natural 6 To Wound in combat, enemy regular/heavy infantry and light/heavy cavalry get no armour or Regeneration save (Ward saves allowed). Not usable on auto-wounding attacks.
summaries.Grinding Wheels: The Steam Tank's Stomp attacks have AP -2 (not against behemoths); it treats low linear obstacles as open terrain.
summaries.Levitating Shield: Infantry only. Counts as a shield, and may be used together with a weapon that Requires Two Hands.
summaries.Prayer of the Damned: Command sub-phase: pass a Leadership test to give this character and joined unit, until your next turn, a 6+ Ward save (5+ against Fear/Terror enemies in combat).
summaries.Storm Call: Until your next turn, target enemy unit takes -1 Movement and -1 Initiative (min 1). Casting it removes any earlier Hex on the target.
summaries.Summon Elemental Spirit: Remains in play: a 3" template (difficult terrain, blocks line of sight) within 15", scattering D6" each Start of Turn. Enemies it moves over or that move through take D3+3 Strength 4 AP-1 hits.
summaries.Wind Blast: Target enemy unit suffers D3+3 Strength 5 AP-1 hits, then must Give Ground.
summaries.Order of the Knights Panther: Must use lances. May re-roll one natural 1 on the Charge roll, and gains Hatred (Warriors of Chaos, Beastmen Brayherds & Daemons). Order Grand/Chapter Masters and Inner Circle Knights also gain Magic Resistance (-1).
summaries.Ring of Taal: One use: cast the Oaken Shield spell (Lore of Battle Magic) as a Bound spell with Power Level 3.
summaries.Usirian’s Reaper: After deployment, name an enemy character: Apophas re-rolls failed To Hit/To Wound against it, and his hits on it count as Magical Attacks.
summaries.Lore Familiar: The owner chooses their spells from their lore (including the signature spell) instead of generating them randomly.
summaries.The Big Red Raggedy Flag: The unit gains +1 Weapon Skill (max 10) and an extra +1 combat result point.
summaries.Da Spider Banner: The unit gains Poisoned Attacks; if it already has them, it wounds automatically on a natural 5 or 6 To Hit instead of just 6.
summaries.Virtue of Heroism: Unless using a magic weapon, a model with this Virtue gains the Killing Blow and Monster Slayer special rules.
summaries.Aura of the Lady: Any unit this character has joined gains the Magical Attacks special rule.
summaries.Fireball: The target enemy unit suffers 2D6 Strength 4 hits, each with an AP of -.
summaries.Curse of Arrow Attraction: Until your next Start of Turn sub-phase, you may re-roll any rolls To Hit of a natural 1 when shooting at the target enemy unit.
summaries.Arcane Urgency: If the target friendly unit is not fleeing and has moved during this Movement phase, it may immediately move again.
summaries.Oaken Shield: Until your next Start of Turn sub-phase, the caster and any unit they have joined gain 5+ Ward save against any wounds suffered.
summaries.Hammerhand: A single enemy unit the caster is engaged in combat with suffers 2D3 Strength 4 hits, each with an AP of -2.
summaries.Monster Hunter's Tapestry: Enemy units may not make Stomp attacks against a unit carrying the Monster Hunter's Tapestry
summaries.Unquiet Spirits: The target enemy unit suffers 3D6 Strength 2 hits, with no armour save permitted (Ward and Regeneration saves can be attempted as normal).
summaries.Arrows of Asaph: Units with this special rule never apply any modifiers to their rolls To Hit when shooting, regardless of the source of the modifier.
summaries.Howdah: To represent its howdah and crew, a behemoth with this special rule has a split profile and follows both the ‘Split Profile (Chariots)’ and ‘Firing Platform’ rules (see page 194).
summaries.\"Hold The Line!\": This character and any unit they have joined automatically passes any Panic tests they are required to make.
summaries.Griffon Standard: When determining your combat result, a unit carrying the Griffon Standard can claim a Rank Bonus of +2 for each extra rank behind the first, rather than the usual +1.
summaries.Soulfire: A single enemy unit this model is engaged in combat with immediately suffers D6 Strength 3 hits, each with an AP of -2.
summaries.Flaming Sword: A single enemy unit the caster is engaged in combat with suffers D6+1 Strength 3 hits, each with and AP of -.
summaries.Plague of Rust: Until your next Start of Turn sub-phase, the target enemy unit suffers a -2 modifier to its armour value.
summaries.Rune of Fear: A unit carrying a standard inscribed with the Rune of Fear gains the [Fear] special rule.
summaries.Scarab Prince: Should Prince Apophas lose his last Wound, before his model is removed from play, all enemy units within 2D6" of him suffer 2D6 Strength 2 hits with an AP of -1.
summaries.Fanatic Ball & Chain: Any unit (friend or foe) that is moved through by a moving Fanatic, or that moves through a Fanatic, suffers D6 Strength 5 hits, each with an AP of -3.
summaries.Da Angry Ladz Flag: A unit carrying Da Angry Ladz Flag gains the Frenzy special rule.

learned.Imperial Banner: All friendly units within the Command range of the model carrying the Imperial Banner roll 3D6 when making a [Fear], Panic or [Terror] test and discard the highest result.
learned.Ruby Ring of Ruin: The wielder of the Ruby Ring of Ruin can cast the Fireball spell from the Lore of Battle Magic (as described on page 321) as a Bound spell, with a Power Level of 1.
learned.The Blazing Banner: A unit carrying the Blazing Banner gains the Flaming Attacks special rule.
learned.Order of the Knights of Morr: Models belonging to the Order of the Knights of Morr: - Gain the Hatred (Undead models) special rule.
